{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27589","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27589/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27589/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27589/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27589","id":2597696829,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86a1bU9","number":27589,"title":"BUG: The order of importing numpy and open3d affects the loading of libGL.","user":{"login":"wzy-99","id":44768324,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ0NzY4MzI0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/44768324?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99","html_url":"https://github.com/wzy-99","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wzy-99/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-18T14:29:32Z","updated_at":"2024-10-18T14:29:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

In a simple GUI visualization code, if I import open3d before numpy, the program runs normally. However, if I import numpy first and then open3d, there will be an OpenGL loading error.

Here is my code:

1. Import numpy first:
```python
import numpy as np
import open3d as o3d

points = np.random.rand(1000, 3)
pcd = o3d.geometry.PointCloud()
pcd.points = o3d.utility.Vector3dVector(points)

o3d.visualization.draw_geometries([pcd])
```  
The program encounters an error:
```bash
libGL error: MESA-LOADER: failed to open swrast: /usr/lib/dri/swrast_dri.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory (search paths /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/dri:\$${ORIGIN}/dri:/usr/lib/dri, suffix _dri)
libGL error: failed to load driver: swrast
[Open3D WARNING] GLFW Error: GLX: Failed to create context: GLXBadFBConfig
[Open3D WARNING] Failed to create window
[Open3D WARNING] [DrawGeometries] Failed creating OpenGL window.
```
2. Import open3d first:
```python
import open3d as o3d
import numpy as np

points = np.random.rand(1000, 3)
pcd = o3d.geometry.PointCloud()
pcd.points = o3d.utility.Vector3dVector(points)

o3d.visualization.draw_geometries([pcd])
```
The program runs normally.

My environment is WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS.

My guess is that importing numpy first might change some environment settings, leading to issues with OpenGL loading. However, I don't know how to verify this.

Can you help me solve this problem? Or provide some ideas on how to resolve it?

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import open3d as o3d

points = np.random.rand(1000, 3)
pcd = o3d.geometry.PointCloud()
pcd.points = o3d.utility.Vector3dVector(points)

o3d.visualization.draw_geometries([pcd])
```


### Error message:

```shell
libGL error: MESA-LOADER: failed to open swrast: /usr/lib/dri/swrast_dri.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory (search paths /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/dri:\$${ORIGIN}/dri:/usr/lib/dri, suffix _dri)
libGL error: failed to load driver: swrast
[Open3D WARNING] GLFW Error: GLX: Failed to create context: GLXBadFBConfig
[Open3D WARNING] Failed to create window
[Open3D WARNING] [DrawGeometries] Failed creating OpenGL window.
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.0.1
3.10.15 (main, Oct  3 2024, 07:27:34) [GCC 11.2.0]

### Runtime Environment:

Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null},{"id":2124388074,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMTI0Mzg4MDc0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/Static%20typing","name":"Static typing","color":"fcff6b","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2022-08-17T22:06:11Z","updated_at":"2022-08-25T02:49:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

np.cross appears to have a return type of `NoReturn` when dealing with `_ArrayLikeBool_co` type, and this `NoReturn` type is incompatible with type checking in Pylance, and VSCode determines that all code after a call to np.cross is unreachable. 

This has been mentioned in a Pylance issue here (https://github.com/microsoft/pylance-release/issues/3195), but they recommended mentioning it with NumPy. 

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np

x = np.array([1,2,3])
y = np.array([2,3,4])

z = np.cross(x, y)
# This code is determined as unreachable by VSCode due to a NoReturn type
print(z)
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### NumPy/Python version information:

1.23.2 3.9.12 (main, Jun 12 2022, 13:39:05)
[GCC 9.4.0]

### Context for the issue:

My code uses np.cross in many places, as well as extensive type-checking. It would be very nice if the return types could be cleaned up to improve compatibility with VSCode/Pylance/Pyright, because right now all of my code is grayed-out
